extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs_hard/_judge_prompts_blind/item-cda4843b60fc.txt

The culprit is transition t16: on send_attempt if (opted_out and stage >= 4): sent := true. This sets sent true while opted_out is true, so the violation hinges on whether stage==4 is reachable with opted_out==true.

opted_out is set true by t1 (optout) and is never reset to false by any transition. The stage-advancing transitions (t3 stage0->1 on key_2, t6 stage1->2 on key_1, t9 stage2->3 on key_1, t12 stage3->4 on key_0) have no guard on opted_out, so they fire regardless. Thus stage can climb to 4 after opting out.

Trace (initial: opted_out=false, sent=false, stage=0):
1. optout -> t1: opted_out=true, sent=false, stage=0
2. key_2 -> t3 (stage==0): stage=1, sent=false
3. key_1 -> t6 (stage==1): stage=2, sent=false
4. key_1 -> t9 (stage==2): stage=3, sent=false
5. key_0 -> t12 (stage==3): stage=4, sent=false
6. send_attempt: t2 disabled (requires not opted_out), t15 disabled (requires stage<4, stage=4), t16 enabled (opted_out=true and stage>=4) -> sent=true

Resulting state: opted_out=true AND sent=true, which violates "opted_out implies not sent". The counterexample uses 6 events, within the 7-event bound.
